meta.title: Juan Fdez | Data & Markets
meta.description: Data-driven research tools for markets, risk and fintech.
meta.keywords: Data Engineering, Financial Markets, Risk Analytics, Fintech, Python, AWS, Equity Research, Portfolio Risk, Financial Data

hero.name: Juan Fdez
hero.headline: I build data-driven research tools for markets, risk and fintech.
hero.description: From company filings to valuation models, dashboards and risk insights — built in public with Python, cloud and financial data.

focus.section_desc: Active areas of development for this public build.

items.title: Portfolio Foundation
items.desc: Setting up the core structure, workflows and tooling for future public projects.

items.title: Research Workflow
items.desc: Defining the data collection, modeling and publishing process step by step.

items.title: Data Tools
items.desc: Building reusable Python modules and pipelines for financial data processing.

items.title: Public Build
items.desc: Documenting the entire process openly. Every project ships with code.

framework.section_desc: A structured approach to turning raw financial data into clear, reproducible analysis.

steps.title: Collect official data
steps.desc: Annual reports, regulatory filings, central bank releases and public financial datasets.

steps.title: Clean and structure
steps.desc: Pandas pipelines to normalize, validate and organize data into analysis-ready formats.

steps.title: Model scenarios
steps.desc: Valuation models, risk metrics and scenario analysis across different assumptions.

steps.title: Identify risks
steps.desc: Stress tests, sensitivity analysis and explicit risk documentation before conclusions.

steps.title: Publish clear insights
steps.desc: Results as notes, dashboards or interactive tools — clear, reproducible and open.

principles.section_desc: A set of rules that guide every project and analysis.

items.title: Official data first
items.desc: Primary sources over secondary. Filings and regulatory data over aggregators.

items.title: Scenarios over predictions
items.desc: No point forecasts. Every analysis considers a range of outcomes.

items.title: Risks before upside
items.desc: What can go wrong matters more than what can go right.

items.title: Clear assumptions
items.desc: Every model input is explicit, documented and challengeable.

items.title: Build in public
items.desc: Code, process and results are open. No black boxes.

data_sources.section_desc: The types of data that feed every project.

tech_stack.section_desc: The tools I use to collect, process, model and publish financial data.

roadmap.section_desc: Phases for this public build. Everything is evolving.

phases.title: Foundation
phases.desc: Website, workflow and core structure in place.

phases.title: Interactive tools
phases.desc: Build and deploy reusable financial tools.

cta.headline: Follow the build.
cta.desc: This is a public lab. Projects, code and research are published as they are built throughout the year.

about_page.title: Juan Fernández
about_page.bio: I'm a Data Engineering student focused on financial markets, risk analytics and fintech. This site is my public lab: a place to build and publish research workflows, data tools, dashboards and clear financial insights while documenting the process.
focus_items: Building public projects on markets and risk
Turning financial data into clear analysis
Connecting data engineering with markets and risk
about_page.disclaimer: All content is for educational purposes only. Not investment advice.

footer.disclaimer: Research for educational purposes only. No investment advice.
footer.built_with: Built with Next.js, financial data and curiosity.
